steps.title: Vertex shortcut: x-intercepts are symmetric about the vertex x-coordinate
steps.content: Vertex is $(-3, 6)$, so the axis of symmetry is $x = -3$. One x-intercept is at $x = -\frac{17}{4}$.

steps.title: Mirror the known root across the axis
steps.content:
Other intercept $= 2h - r_1 = 2(-3) - \left(-\dfrac{17}{4}\right) = -6 + \dfrac{17}{4} = -\dfrac{24}{4} + \dfrac{17}{4} = -\dfrac{7}{4}$.
The other x-intercept is $\left(-\dfrac{7}{4}, 0\right)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
